title: The Kid and the Wolf
lines: A young Kid, separated from the flock, was grazing alone when he saw a Wolf in the distance.
The Kid was not afraid, but asked the Wolf, 'Why are you always chasing us?'
'Because I am hungry," replied the Wolf.
'If you give me a good reason not to eat you, perhaps I will spare your life.'
The Kid thought for a moment and said, 'I could never satisfy your hunger.'
'But if you let me go, I will bring you a larger prey.'
The Wolf agreed, and the Kid escaped to safety.
moral: Wit can save you when strength cannot.
